full_response:
We want to perform an active, right-handed rotation through an angle θ about the z–axis (i.e. in the x–y plane) with the origin as the pivot.  

1. Rotation matrix.  
   For a rotation by θ about the z–axis the 3×3 matrix is  

   R_z(θ)=
   ⎛⎜⎝ cosθ   −sinθ   0  
           sinθ    cosθ   0  
              0        0     1 ⎞⎟⎠ .

2. Apply the matrix to the column vector of the initial coordinates (x₀, y₀, z₀):

   ⎛x⎞   ⎛ cosθ  −sinθ  0⎞ ⎛x₀⎞   = ⎛ x₀ cosθ − y₀ sinθ ⎞  
   ⎜y⎟ = ⎜ sinθ   cosθ  0⎟ ⎜y₀⎟   = ⎜ x₀ sinθ + y₀ cosθ ⎟  
   ⎝z⎠   ⎝  0       0     1⎠ ⎝z₀⎠   = ⎝         z₀            ⎠ .

3. Result.  
   The z–coordinate is unchanged because the axis of rotation is the z-axis.

\boxed{\,(x,y,z)=\bigl(x_{0}\cos\theta - y_{0}\sin\theta,\; x_{0}\sin\theta + y_{0}\cos\theta,\; z_{0}\bigr)\,}

Confidence: 98%
